title: Feedback log persistence is not deployment-safe

evidence.quote:
const DISCORD_WEBHOOK = process.env.FEEDBACK_DISCORD_WEBHOOK ?? "";
const LOG_FILE        = path.join(process.cwd(), "feedback.json");

## Deploy on Vercel

The easiest way to deploy your Next.js app is to use the [Vercel Platform](https://vercel.com/new?utm_medium=default-template&filter=next.js&utm_source=create-next-app&utm_campaign=create-next-app-readme) from the creators of Next.js.

Check out our [Next.js deployment documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying) for more details.

reasoning: The route treats process.cwd()/feedback.json as durable storage and suppresses write failures. That is fragile for production Next deployments where app filesystems may be read-only, ephemeral, or split across instances. In those environments feedback can appear accepted while the local log is missing or partial, especially when FEEDBACK_DISCORD_WEBHOOK is not configured.
reproduction: Deploy the app in an environment where the application directory is read-only or instances do not share a filesystem, then POST feedback with no webhook configured. The route still returns success even though the local persistence path cannot reliably retain the submission.
recommendation: Use an external durable store for feedback, or explicitly make local file logging development-only and return a failure when no durable sink is available. Do not swallow persistence errors for the only configured storage path.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for production-like filesystem behavior or for the case where the webhook is absent and file persistence fails.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock fs.writeFileSync to throw with FEEDBACK_DISCORD_WEBHOOK unset and assert the route does not return a misleading success response.
minimumFixScope: Move feedback persistence to a deployment-safe store or gate local file logging to development while reporting storage failures correctly.
